topic: The Terrifying Math of Exponential Asteroid Impacts
title: Why Small Changes in Size Make Asteroid Impacts So Dangerous

lesson.learn:
Asteroid impacts get far more dangerous as size and speed increase because kinetic energy rises with mass and with the square of velocity. That means a modest jump in either factor can produce a much larger explosion on impact. The result is not a simple step up in damage; it is an exponential jump in risk.

The Tunguska event and the Chelyabinsk airburst show the difference between a dramatic regional blast and a planet-ending disaster. Planetary defense is therefore less about panic and more about early detection, orbit tracking, and the ability to nudge a threat before it arrives.
lesson.simpler: Bigger and faster asteroids release way more energy. That is why a small change in size can turn a warning into a disaster.
lesson.keyTakeaway: Asteroid risk grows explosively because impact energy scales with mass and speed.
lesson.deeperDive:
A dense object moving at cosmic speed carries enormous kinetic energy, and Earth can only absorb so much of it safely. If an asteroid enters the atmosphere, it may break apart in an airburst, but a larger one can reach the ground and carve a crater. That is why astronomers focus on cataloging near-Earth objects and testing deflection methods like DART.

The math also explains why preparedness matters. If we find a hazardous object years or decades early, a small course change can make the difference between a miss and a collision.
lesson.funFact: Impact energy rises with speed squared.

people: Lindley Johnson - NASA planetary defense advocate
Kelly Fast - near-Earth object program leader
A. A. Stern - planetary science researcher
places: NASA Planetary Defense Coordination Office - asteroid tracking
Arecibo/observatory networks - sky survey and radar work
years: 2022 - DART successfully changes an asteroid moonlet
concepts: Kinetic energy - the energy of motion
Near-Earth object - an asteroid or comet close to Earth
Planetary defense - finding and reducing impact risks
